questions.question: What is 'Universal Adult Franchise'?
questions.correct_answer: The right of all adult citizens to vote regardless of their wealth or social status
questions.explanation: It is a central aspect of all democracies and is based on the idea of equality.

questions.question: Which document recognizes every person as equal in India?
questions.explanation: The Constitution states that every individual in the country, including male and female from all castes, religions, and backgrounds, is recognized as equal.

questions.question: What are the four provisions in the Constitution regarding equality?
questions.correct_answer: Equality before law, no discrimination, access to public places, and abolition of untouchability
questions.explanation: These laws ensure that every citizen is treated with dignity and has equal opportunities.

questions.question: What was the Midday Meal scheme and which state started it first?
questions.correct_answer: A program to provide cooked lunch to children in government schools; started by Tamil Nadu
questions.explanation: In 2001, the Supreme Court asked all state governments to begin this program within six months.

questions.question: How does the Midday Meal scheme promote equality?
questions.correct_answer: By making children of all castes sit and eat together
questions.explanation: It reduces caste prejudices and helps poor students who can now attend school without being hungry.

questions.question: Who is Rosa Parks and why is she significant?
questions.correct_answer: An African-American woman whose refusal to give up her bus seat started the Civil Rights Movement
questions.explanation: Her act on 1 December 1955 started a massive agitation against the unequal ways in which African-Americans were treated in the USA.
questions.source_section: Issues of Equality in Other Democracies

questions.question: What did the Civil Rights Act of 1964 prohibit?
questions.correct_answer: Discrimination on the basis of race, religion, or national origin
questions.explanation: It was a landmark law in the United States that aimed to end segregation and unequal treatment.
questions.source_section: Issues of Equality in Other Democracies

questions.question: What is the 'Ansari' case an example of?
questions.explanation: The Ansaris were denied an apartment for rent despite being able to pay, simply because of their religious identity.

questions.question: What does the term 'Dignity' refer to?
questions.correct_answer: Thinking of oneself and other persons as worthy of respect
questions.explanation: When persons are treated unequally, their dignity is violated.

questions.question: What is the meaning of 'Dalit'?
questions.correct_answer: A term meaning 'broken' used by lower castes to address themselves
questions.explanation: It points to how they were, and continue to be, seriously discriminated against.

notes.title: Article 15
notes.content: A part of the Constitution that prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth.

notes.title: Role of Government
notes.content: The government tries to implement equality through laws and through government programs/schemes to help disadvantaged communities.

notes.title: Challenge of Democracy
notes.content: No country is completely democratic; there are always communities and individuals pushing for more equality and recognition.

facts.fact: Omprakash Valmiki is a famous Dalit writer known for his autobiography 'Joothan'.
B.R. Ambedkar said that self-respect is the most vital factor in life; without it, man is a cipher.
The Civil Rights Act also stated that all schools would be open to African-American children.
facts.source_section: Issues of Equality in Other Democracies
